tasks.name: Save the earliest 4th-grade math club that ends by 4:30 PM and costs under $40/month
tasks.description: Use the after-school programs page to filter for 4th-grade math/science clubs that cost $40/month or less and end by 4:30 PM, then sort by start time and save the earliest qualifying program.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Activities" item in the top navigation bar.
Select "After-School Programs" from the Activities dropdown menu.
On the programs page, click the grade filter dropdown and choose "4th Grade".
In the category or subject filter section, check the option for "Math & Science" to limit results to math or science clubs.
In the fee filter area, click the maximum fee field and type "40" so only programs costing $40 or less per month are shown.
Open the time filter controls and set the "End Time" option to "4:30 PM or earlier".
Above the list of programs, click the sort dropdown and select "Start Time – Earliest First".
In the sorted results, click the heart or "Save" icon on the first program card to save the earliest 4th-grade math club that meets the cost and end-time criteria.

tasks.name: Plan 3 vegetarian, nut-free school lunches for the week of March 9
tasks.description: Use the lunch menu to filter for vegetarian and nut-free options for the week of March 9 and add lunch choices for exactly three different days to the meal planner.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Families" link in the main navigation.
Choose "Lunch Menu" from the Families submenu.
On the lunch menu page, use the week selector dropdown and choose "Week of March 9".
Click the "Dietary Filters" button and check both "Vegetarian" and "Nut-Free" so only vegetarian, nut-free items are shown.
Scroll through the daily menus for Monday through Friday of the selected week to view the filtered options.

Starting from Monday, locate vegetarian, nut-free main entrée options and, for the first three different school days that offer a qualifying entrée, click the "Add to Meal Planner" button next to that entrée.
After adding three lunches, click the "My Meal Planner" icon or link in the page header to open the planner page.
On the meal planner page, scroll through the week of March 9 to view the three vegetarian, nut-free lunch selections scheduled on three different days.

tasks.name: Save the earliest bus route to Oak Street arriving before 7:45 AM
tasks.description: Find bus routes that serve Oak Street for elementary students, filter for buses arriving before 7:45 AM, sort by arrival time, and save the earliest route to your saved routes list.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Transportation" item in the main navigation bar.
Select the "Bus Routes & Times" option from the Transportation page or submenu.
In the route search box, click inside the field, type "Oak Street", and press Enter to search for routes serving that street.
Use the grade level filter controls and select "K-5 (Elementary)" so only elementary routes are shown.
Open the "Arrival Time" filter and choose the option labeled "Arrives before 7:45 AM".
Click the sort dropdown above the results list and select "Arrival Time – Earliest First".
In the sorted list of matching routes, click on the first route entry to open its route details page.
On the route details page, click the "Add to My Routes" or "Save Route" button to save this earliest qualifying bus route for Oak Street.

tasks.name: Save two April evening events for 1st-grade families between 5–8 PM
tasks.description: Use the calendar to filter April events for 1st-grade families that start between 5:00 PM and 8:00 PM, then save the first two matching events to your saved events list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Calendar" link in the main navigation bar.
On the calendar page, open the month dropdown and select "April".
Click the "Filter" button and set the "Audience" filter to "1st Grade Families".
Within the same filter panel, set the "Time of Day" or "Start Time" filter to show events starting between "5:00 PM" and "8:00 PM".
Click the "Apply" button to update the event list with the chosen audience and time filters.

In the filtered event list view, right-click or use the appropriate action (such as Ctrl+click) on the title of the first listed matching event and choose "Open link in new tab" to open its detail page.
Repeat the same action on the title of the second listed matching event so that its detail page also opens in a new tab.
Switch to each of the two event detail tabs and click the "Save Event" or "Add to My Events" button on each page to save both April evening events for 1st-grade families.

tasks.name: Contact the most experienced 3rd-grade math teacher via the staff directory
tasks.description: Use the staff directory to filter for 3rd-grade math teachers, sort by years of experience, and send a message through the contact form to the teacher with the most years of experience.
steps: On the homepage, click the "About" item in the main navigation.
Select "Staff Directory" from the About dropdown menu.
On the staff directory page, use the grade filter and select "3rd Grade" from the list of grades.
Use the subject or role filter and choose "Math" so only 3rd-grade math teachers are displayed.
Click the sort dropdown and select "Years of Experience – Most to Least" to order teachers by experience.
Click on the first teacher in the sorted list to open that teacher's profile page.
On the teacher profile page, click the "Contact Teacher" or "Send Message" button.

In the contact form that appears, type "Jordan Lee" in the Parent/Guardian Name field, "Mia Lee" in the Student Name field, and "parent@example.com" in the Email field, then enter a brief message asking about math homework expectations in the Message box.
Click the "Send Message" button to submit the form to the most experienced 3rd-grade math teacher.

tasks.name: Bookmark the 2nd-grade homework policy limiting nightly homework to 30 minutes
tasks.description: Navigate to the homework policies, filter for 2nd grade, go to the homework time guidelines section that states a 30-minute nightly limit, and bookmark that section for quick access later.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Academics" link in the main navigation.
Choose "Homework & Grading Policies" from the Academics menu or page options.
On the policies page, locate the grade-level filter and select "2nd Grade" so the policies shown apply to 2nd grade.
In the section navigation or table of contents sidebar, click the link labeled "Homework Time Guidelines" (or the equivalent section title).
Scroll within the Homework Time Guidelines section until you reach the subsection that lists nightly homework time limits.
Using the mouse, click and drag to highlight the sentence or paragraph that mentions the "30 minutes" nightly homework limit for 2nd grade.
Near the heading of this section, click the "Bookmark this section" icon or link to save this 2nd-grade homework time policy to your personal bookmarks within the site.
Click the "My Bookmarks" icon or link in the header to open your bookmarks page and scroll until you see the entry for the 2nd-grade homework time guidelines section.

tasks.name: Create a 2nd-grade supply checklist with four optional items costing $5 or less
tasks.description: Open the 2nd-grade school supply list, show only optional items, sort them by estimated cost, and add the first four items that cost $5.00 or less to your supply checklist.
steps: On the homepage, click the "Families" option in the main navigation.
Select "School Supply Lists" from the Families submenu.
On the supply lists page, use the grade selector dropdown and choose "2nd Grade".
Activate the "Optional Items Only" toggle or checkbox so that only optional supplies are displayed.
Click the sort dropdown above the list and select "Estimated Cost – Low to High" to order items by price.
Scroll down the optional items list and, starting from the top, look for items with an estimated cost of $5.00 or less displayed next to them.
For the first four items you encounter that each have a cost of $5.00 or less, click the "Add to Checklist" button next to each item.
Click the "My Supply Checklist" link or icon in the header to open your checklist and scroll through the list to view the four selected optional items under $5.00.

tasks.name: Submit an interest form for a July summer camp running 9 AM–3 PM under $150/week
tasks.description: Use the summer camp programs page to filter July sessions that run from 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM and cost no more than $150 per week, then open the first matching session and submit an interest form with sample contact details.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Programs" item in the main navigation bar.
Select "Summer Camps" from the Programs dropdown menu.
On the Summer Camps page, open the month filter and choose "July" so only July sessions are shown.
Use the "Daily Schedule" or "Hours" filter and select the option labeled "9:00 AM–3:00 PM".
In the fee or cost filter area, click the maximum weekly fee field and type "150" so only camps costing $150 per week or less are included.
Click the "Apply Filters" button to update the list to July camps that run 9:00 AM–3:00 PM and cost $150/week or less.
In the filtered results, click on the first camp session card to open its detailed information page.
On the camp detail page, click the "Register Interest" or "Request Info" button to open the interest form.

In the form, enter "Alex Rivera" in the Child's Name field, select "3rd Grade" in the Upcoming Grade dropdown, type "Sam Rivera" in the Parent/Guardian Name field, enter "555-123-4567" in the Phone Number field, and choose one of the listed July session weeks from the Preferred Session dropdown.
Click the "Submit" button to send the interest form for the selected July camp session that meets the time and cost criteria.
